vision: A lightweight, distraction-free mobile task management tool that helps individuals efficiently organize and complete their daily tasks.

epics.name: Project Foundation
epics.description: Establish the core application architecture, project setup, and basic navigation structure to enable all subsequent feature development.

stories.title: Set up mobile project structure
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want the app to be available on my mobile device, so that I can manage tasks on the go.
acceptance_criteria: Given the development team starts work When the project is initialized Then a cross-platform mobile project structure exists for iOS and Android
Given the project is set up When the app is built Then it compiles successfully for both iOS and Android platforms
stories.notes: Walking skeleton foundation - Unity project setup with basic scene structure

stories.title: Implement basic app navigation shell
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want to see a clean main screen when I open the app, so that I can immediately access my tasks.
acceptance_criteria: Given the user launches the app When the app loads Then a main task list screen is displayed
Given the main screen is displayed When the user views it Then basic UI elements are visible and responsive
stories.notes: Walking skeleton - basic UI shell implementation

stories.title: Design core UI visual assets
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want the app to have a clean visual design, so that using it feels pleasant and distraction-free.
acceptance_criteria: Given the UI needs visual assets When assets are created Then icons, buttons, and UI elements follow a consistent visual style
Given the visual assets are complete When integrated into the app Then the interface appears polished and cohesive
stories.notes: UI/UX visual design, icons, color scheme, typography assets

epics.name: Local Data Persistence
epics.description: Implement local storage capabilities to ensure task data persists on the device and is available offline.

stories.title: Implement local data storage system
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want my tasks to be saved on my device, so that I don't lose them when I close the app.
acceptance_criteria: Given the user creates a task When the app is closed and reopened Then the task is still visible in the list
Given the user has existing tasks When the device is restarted Then all tasks are restored from local storage
stories.notes: Walking skeleton - local persistence using PlayerPrefs or local database

stories.title: Enable offline task operations
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want to manage my tasks without internet connection, so that I can stay productive anywhere.
acceptance_criteria: Given the device has no internet connection When the user creates, completes, or deletes a task Then the operation succeeds locally
Given the user is offline When viewing the task list Then all locally stored tasks are displayed
stories.notes: Ensure all core operations work without network dependency

epics.name: Task Creation
epics.description: Enable users to create new tasks with essential details including title and due date.

stories.title: Create task with title input
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want to create a new task with a title, so that I can capture what needs to be done.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the main screen When I tap the add task button Then a task creation form appears
Given I am on the task creation form When I enter a title and submit Then the new task appears in my task list
Given I am creating a task When I leave the title empty Then the form prevents submission with an error message

stories.title: Add due date to task
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want to set a due date when creating a task, so that I know when it needs to be completed.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am creating a new task When I tap the due date field Then a date picker is displayed
Given I select a date from the picker When I save the task Then the due date is stored and displayed with the task
Given I am creating a task When I don't select a due date Then the task is created without a due date
stories.notes: Due date is optional based on acceptance criteria

epics.name: Task Completion
epics.description: Allow users to mark tasks as complete to track their progress and feel accomplishment.

stories.title: Mark task as complete
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want to mark a task as complete, so that I can track my progress and feel accomplished.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have an incomplete task When I tap the completion checkbox or button Then the task is marked as complete
Given a task is marked complete When I view the task list Then the task displays a visual indicator showing it is complete
Given a task is complete When I tap the completion control again Then the task is marked as incomplete
stories.notes: Toggle behavior allows correction of accidental completions

stories.title: Create completion visual feedback
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want clear visual feedback when completing a task, so that I feel rewarded for my progress.
acceptance_criteria: Given I complete a task When the completion is registered Then a satisfying visual animation plays
Given a task is complete When viewing the list Then completed tasks have distinct visual styling
stories.notes: Animation and visual effects for completion feedback

epics.name: Task Deletion
epics.description: Provide ability to remove tasks from the list when they are no longer needed.

stories.title: Delete task from list
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want to delete a task, so that I can remove items that are no longer relevant.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have a task in my list When I initiate the delete action Then the task is removed from the list
Given I delete a task When I view the task list Then the deleted task no longer appears
Given I initiate delete When prompted to confirm Then I can cancel to keep the task
stories.notes: Include confirmation to prevent accidental deletion

stories.title: Implement swipe-to-delete gesture
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want to swipe a task to delete it, so that I can quickly remove tasks with a natural gesture.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am viewing my task list When I swipe left on a task Then a delete option is revealed
Given the delete option is revealed When I tap delete or complete the swipe Then the task is deleted
stories.notes: Enhanced UX feature - mobile-native interaction pattern

epics.name: Task List Viewing
epics.description: Display tasks in an organized list format with filtering capabilities to help users focus on relevant tasks.

stories.title: Display all tasks in list
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want to see all my tasks in a list, so that I have an overview of everything I need to do.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have created multiple tasks When I view the main screen Then all tasks are displayed in a scrollable list
Given each task in the list When displayed Then it shows the task title and due date if set
Given I have no tasks When I view the main screen Then an empty state message is displayed

stories.title: Filter to show incomplete tasks only
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want to filter my list to show only incomplete tasks, so that I can focus on what still needs to be done.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have both complete and incomplete tasks When I apply the incomplete filter Then only incomplete tasks are shown
Given the incomplete filter is active When I view the list Then complete tasks are hidden
stories.notes: Essential filter for daily task management

stories.title: Filter to show complete tasks only
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want to filter my list to show only complete tasks, so that I can review what I have accomplished.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have both complete and incomplete tasks When I apply the complete filter Then only complete tasks are shown
Given the complete filter is active When I view the list Then incomplete tasks are hidden
stories.notes: Supports sense of accomplishment per user needs

stories.title: Clear all filters view option
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want to clear filters and see all tasks, so that I can return to the full task overview.
acceptance_criteria: Given a filter is currently active When I select show all or clear filter Then all tasks are displayed regardless of completion status
Given no filter is active When I view the list Then both complete and incomplete tasks are visible

epics.name: Platform Deployment
epics.description: Prepare and deploy the application to iOS and Android app stores.

stories.title: Build and deploy iOS app
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want to download the app from the iOS App Store, so that I can use it on my iPhone or iPad.
acceptance_criteria: Given the app is complete When submitted to Apple Then it passes App Store review requirements
Given the app is approved When a user searches the App Store Then the app is discoverable and downloadable
stories.notes: iOS build configuration, signing, and store submission

stories.title: Build and deploy Android app
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want to download the app from the Google Play Store, so that I can use it on my Android device.
acceptance_criteria: Given the app is complete When submitted to Google Then it passes Play Store review requirements
Given the app is approved When a user searches the Play Store Then the app is discoverable and downloadable
stories.notes: Android build configuration, signing, and store submission

stories.title: Create app store visual assets
stories.user_story: As a Individual User, I want to see appealing screenshots and icon in the app store, so that I understand what the app does before downloading.
acceptance_criteria: Given the app is ready for submission When preparing store listings Then app icon, screenshots, and promotional graphics are created
Given store assets are created When viewed on store pages Then they accurately represent the app functionality and design
stories.notes: App icons, store screenshots, feature graphics
